course_description:
This course equips students with fundamental and transferable skills in conducting research both within and outside of their disciplinary areas through a series of workshops designed to build on their scientific research knowledge. Opportunities will be provided for students to gain first-hand experience of research activities outside of their own disciplinary area in the Faculty of Science through field trips and site visits, and students will be required to work in multidisciplinary groups for workshop activities. This course is designed to complement and enhance students' experience of their own research project. Students should complete this course in their first semester of Honours year.

Students in this course will work in multidisciplinary groups, with peers doing Honours in a variety of Schools across the Faculty of Science.

In the School of Mathematics and Physics we are committed to creating an inclusive and empowering learning environment for all students. We value and respect the diverse range of experiences our students bring to their education, and we believe that this diversity is crucial for fostering a rich culture of knowledge sharing and meaningful exploration. We hold both students and staff accountable for actively contributing to the establishment of a respectful and supportive learning environment.

Bullying, harassment, and discrimination in any form are strictly against our principles and against UQ Policy, and will not be tolerated. We have developed a suite of resources to assist you in recognising, reporting, and addressing such behaviour. If you have any concerns about your experience in this course, we encourage you to tell a member of the course teaching team, or alternatively contact an SMP Classroom Inclusivity Champion (see Blackboard for contact details). Our Inclusivity Champions are here to listen, to understand your concerns, and to explore potential actions that can be taken to resolve them. Your well-being and a positive learning atmosphere are of utmost importance to us.

restrictions: Restricted to students undertaking science Honours studies: BAdvSc(Hons), BSc(Hons), BSc(Hons) Gatton, BBiotech(Hons), BAgrSc(Hons), BEqSc(Hons), BWSc(Hons), BMath(Hons), BVetTech(Hons), BA(Hons). Students should complete this course in their first semester of Honours year.
Another instance of the same course

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Course activities begin in O-Week.
This course is co-delivered across St Lucia and Gatton campuses.
Most course workshops will be available in-person and online via live-streaming. Most workshops will also be available for later, asynchronous participation by accessing a recording. Synchronous participation in workshops is highly encouraged.
Participation in field trips and 3MT-style presentations (for assessment) is in-person at scheduled times only. Please refer to the course's Blackboard site for details.
Course activities are scheduled to avoid public holidays.
course_aims: This course aims to equip Honours students in disciplines within the Faculty of Science with fundamental research skills including how to develop a research question, design and manage a project, conduct a literature review, deliver presentations to various audiences, incorporate Indigenous perspectives, apply fundamental data analysis tools, and create publication-quality manuscripts. It also aims to provide students with multidisciplinary perspectives and experiences in science research.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop a research-focussed approach to the solution of a new problem, beginning with the development of research questions.
Apply project management techniques to plan and carry out a research project in the allocated time frame, within the available resources and in a safe and ethical manner.
Competently search the scientific literature to critically evaluate previous research.
Offer clear and logical explanations of scientific concepts that may be unfamiliar to the non-specialist through both oral and written communication.
Incorporate Indigenous perspectives in research in a respectful and meaningful way.
Apply fundamental data analysis tools to analyse quantitative and qualitative results.
Be familiar with the contexts of scientific research, and the various research activities that take place within the Faculty’s schools and research centres.

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit this assessment item via the link on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item then the listed penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.

learning_activities.topic: Developmental work with peers Throughout the semester, students will work in multidisciplinary groups to collaboratively develop and refine their research communication products (3MT-style presentations and research pitches). Students will be assigned to teams. Each group will consist of students from multiple Schools in the Faculty of Science. The activity will largely be self-paced and self-managed by student groups. Students organise and run their own meetings. Each team should meet multiple times. Peers in each team will support each other to develop their oral presentations and written research pitches, through a process of discussion and feedback. Students are expected to read drafts and comment on each other's work, and revise their own work for final submission. Group activities (including group meetings, drafts, and peer feedback) will be documented, and submitted as a related assessment item. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From O-week To Week 8 (21 Jul - 21 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Workshops From O-Week (Orientation Week) to two-thirds through the semester, weekly workshops are scheduled. The workshops cover a wide range of topics in research methods (beyond disciplinary knowledge). Topics of the workshops are: Introduction to science research Research literature Project design and management Research ethics and integrity Cultural competency Research field trips Communication of research Quantitative tools For each workshop, students will use a worksheet, and be provided with additional resources through Blackboard and/or presentations. Students will be expected to complete short pre-workshop tasks. Workshop presentations will provide prompts for discussion. Some post-workshop tasks will also be expected to be completed by students. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

3MT-style presentations This item is a learning activity as well as an assessment. Each student will deliver a 3MT-style presentation. Attendance at a '3MT conference' is compulsory. Learning outcomes: L04, L07
